target_emits.name: create_aaa_profile
target_emits.purpose: Create the gateway aaa-profile in the Library. This is the AAA-chain keystone: the normalizer flattens the AOS 8 aaa_prof top-level fields and pre-builds the nested authentication{} (dot1x/mac-auth profile + server-group + role refs) and authorization{} (base role) sub-objects, which key_mappings substitute whole. The referenced dot1x-auth / mac-auth profiles, server-groups, and roles must exist in Central first.
target_emits.iteration_notes: One POST per source aaa_prof record.

target_emits.name: assign_aaa_profile
target_emits.purpose: Assign the aaa-profile to the target scope (gateway). Multi-device-function array packed into a single POST.

config-assignment: {one entry per device_function in target_meta.device_functions: { scope-id, device-function, profile-type='aaa-profile', profile-instance='{name}' }}

target_emits.iteration_notes: One POST; all device-functions share the array.

target_meta.device_function_filtering_rule: The aaa-profile is the gateway-terminated (tunnel-mode) AAA profile; the AOS 8 -> Central migration targets MOBILITY_GW. Operators with AP-direct (bridge) auth fold AAA inline into wlan-ssids instead and can override device_functions.

target_scope_id_resolution.rule: Central scope_id is supplied by the consuming skill at runtime, derived from the AOS 8 aaa_prof record's binding scope.
target_scope_id_resolution.input: Source binding scope (string path / identifier); skill-specific

dependencies.depends_on: central:auth_server + central:server_group (dot1xauth-server-group / macauth-server-group / acct-server-group), central:role (dot1x-default-role / mac-default-role / pre-auth-role), and the dot1x-auth / mac-auth profile translations (authentication.dot1x-auth / mac-auth references). Run all of those first.

depended_by.relation: tunnel-mode wlan-ssids bind to the gateway aaa-profile
depended_by.notes: In the gateway-terminated (tunnel) model, the SSID's AAA is provided by this aaa-profile on the Mobility Gateway (vs the AP-direct/bridge model where auth folds inline into the wlan-ssid). Run aaa-profile before / alongside wlan_ssid.

objects.notes: REST schema 'aaa_prof' (CLI noun: aaa profile). Wraps every value one level deep. References the dot1x-auth / mac-auth SETTINGS profiles (dot1x_auth_profile / mac_auth_profile by name), the dot1x / mac-auth / accounting server-groups (dot1x_server_group / mba_server_group / rad_acct_sg), the per-method default roles (default_user_role / dot1x_default_role / mac_default_role), and the CoA servers (rfc3576_client[]). The normalizer maps the server-group/role/profile refs into the Central authentication{} / authorization{} sub-objects and the rest into top-level fields/flags.

coa_list.notes: rfc3576_client[].rfc3576_server CoA server references. NEEDS-PREVIEW: Central rfc3576-server-list item shape (plain name/IP vs {server-name}) not yet live-verified; CoA is primarily handled on the auth-server side (AUTH_AND_COA), so this is the aaa-profile's server-list view.

authentication.to: body 'authentication' {} (dot1x-auth / dot1x-default-role / dot1xauth-server-group / mac-auth / mac-default-role / macauth-server-group)
authentication.notes: Pre-built by the normalizer from the aaa_prof dot1x/mac-auth profile + server-group + role refs; substituted whole. Omitted when none present.

authorization.notes: Pre-built by the normalizer. AOS 8 default_user_role -> pre-auth-role (semantic-verify: AOS 8 base/initial role vs Central pre-auth-role).

unmapped_fields.from: xml_api_client[] (XML-API servers), dpp-* (DPP onboarding), and authentication.* / authorization.* Central fields with no AOS 8 aaa_prof counterpart (auth-precedence, cloud-auth, critical-auth-role, ubt-fallback-role, etc.)
unmapped_fields.reason: XML-API and DPP have no in-scope AOS 8 -> Central wireless-auth mapping; the extra Central authentication/authorization knobs (cloud-auth, critical roles, UBT) are Central-native features the operator configures post-migration. Leaving them unset = Central defaults.
Clone is a CLI authoring convenience; _flags are AOS 8-internal metadata (consumer filters inherited/default records).

ignored_variants.reason: AOS 8 ships a built-in default aaa-profile. Consumer filters _flags.default / _flags.system / _flags.inherited (at definition scope) BEFORE calling emit_calls, matching the rest of the AAA translations.

draft_notes: v1 translation, Gateway-targeted KEYSTONE. POST /aaa-profile/{name} then config-assignment to MOBILITY_GW. Ties the AAA chain together: references the dot1x/mac-auth profiles, server-groups, roles, and (CoA) servers from the other translations.

TEMPLATE COMPLIANCE: standard Paradigm-B. The one-level value wrapping + the nested authentication{}/authorization{} target sub-objects are pre-built by the normalizer (translations/preprocessing/aos8_aaa_profile.py) and substituted whole (like central:policy's policy-rule[]); the JSON stays thin.

TUNNEL-MODE MODEL: this is the gateway-terminated AAA path (the source tenant is 100% forward_mode=tunnel). In the AP-direct/bridge model Central folds the same auth inline into wlan-ssids instead. See the AAA design notes — the model is termination-dependent.

DEPENDENCY ORDER: auth_server + server_group + role + the dot1x/mac-auth profiles BEFORE this; wlan_ssid references this aaa-profile AFTER. The authentication.dot1x-auth / mac-auth values are the names of the dot1x-auth / mac-auth profiles (separate translations).

ROLE SEMANTICS: default_user_role -> authorization.pre-auth-role is a best-effort map (AOS 8 base/initial role); verify against operator intent. dot1x_default_role / mac_default_role -> authentication.dot1x-default-role / mac-default-role are the post-auth method roles.

NEEDS-PREVIEW: config-assignment profile-type ('aaa-profile'); the rfc3576-server-list item shape; and whether authentication/authorization must be present (vs Central defaulting). Confirm via central_translation_preview against the live aaa_prof records.
Idempotency: SHARED POST for an existing aaa-profile name returns HTTP 409; consumer handles 409 idempotently.
